items.title: WA has enough fuel for May amid 'growing confidence' for June, minister says

children.content: As the Middle East conflict drags on, Minister Amber-Jade Sanderson she's confident about May supplies, and has "growing confidence" for June's outlook.

gallery.altText: Amber-Jade sanderson wears a maroon blazer while sitting in her office, in front of a cabinet with photo frames and plants.

items.title: Dingo sightings near primary school put parents on edge

children.content: "Stressed-out" parents are wondering whether to let their children continue to get around unaccompanied, but wildlife advocates say the animals are not as threatening as their reputation suggests.

image.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

items.title: School traffic warden hit by delivery truck in regional WA

children.content: The man in his 80s was working a school zone crossing in Australind, near Bunbury in WA, when he was hit by the Woolworths truck.

items.title: Man who stabbed father to death with kitchen knife not guilty due to insanity

children.content: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

items.title: Fatal crash on remote gravel road prompts call to limit trucks

children.content: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

children.content: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

items.title: US to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany amid Trump spat with Merz

children.content: The United States is withdrawing 5,000 troops from NATO ally Germany amid a growing a rift between the nations' leaders over the Iran war.

items.title: Trump rejects latest peace proposal but prefers not to 'blast the hell' out of Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump says Iran is "asking for things that I can't agree to", but details of the proposal are unclear. Follow live.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

gallery.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
An Aboriginal man with a grey goatee and wearing a beanie stares through prison bars.
The wayward door to a wrecked caravan, with broken windows.

children.content: Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson are locked in a messy defamation battle involving disparaging social media posts, a "Russian oligarch" and sexual harassment accusations.

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

items.title: WA's rental vacancy half the national average, according to new report

children.content: Western Australians are increasingly being driven towards financial distress and homelessness as the cost of renting continues to surge, according to new data from Anglicare WA.

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

items.title: Officer who laughed at prison death among six sanctioned by WA Police

children.content: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed about his death.

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

items.title: Rare high-end perfume ingredient ambergris washes up on WA beach

children.content: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.
